message:
perf(extensions,dispatcher): allocation-free hostcall dispatch and zero-copy tool execution

Eliminate per-call heap allocations across the entire hostcall dispatch
hot path by replacing .to_ascii_lowercase() + match patterns with
eq_ignore_ascii_case() comparisons and byte-slice matching.

extension_dispatcher.rs:
- Replace policy_lookup_path() match-on-lowercase with eq_ignore_ascii_case
  chain for each capability string, avoiding a String allocation per call
- Replace protocol_error_code() and protocol_error_fallback_reason() match
  arms with if-chain comparisons to avoid lowercase allocation
- Optimize normalized_shadow_op() to build output char-by-char instead of
  allocate+replace, avoiding intermediate String
- Replace clone_payload_object_without_reserved() (generic slice lookup)
  with specialized clone_payload_object_without_key() (single key) and
  clone_payload_object_without_two_keys() (two keys) — eliminates the
  per-call linear scan of reserved keys array
- Inline hostcall_opcode_entropy() to compute Shannon entropy directly
  from byte histogram segments instead of building a temporary Vec<u8>
  concatenation — removes the main per-call allocation on the fast path
- Remove Pin<Box<dyn Future>> boxing from dispatch_batch_amac() and
  dispatch_protocol_message(), using direct async fn instead — avoids
  heap-allocating a boxed future per dispatch
- Pass capability as &str through emit_io_uring_lane_telemetry instead
  of computing it separately
- Use validate_host_call directly instead of validating via message wrapper

extensions.rs:
- Add with_folded_ascii_alnum_token(): zero-allocation byte-slice builder
  using a 64-byte inline buffer with heap fallback, enabling match arms on
  byte literals instead of if-else chains with token_eq_ascii_folded
- Convert all opcode parsers (intern_hostcall_method_atom, parse_tool_opcode,
  parse_session_opcode, parse_events_opcode) to use byte-slice match
- Add EventsHostcallOp enum and parse_events_hostcall_op() to replace
  string matching in dispatch_hostcall_events, moving the unknown-op error
  to a single check before the match
- Add execute_tool_ref() on both NativeRustExtensionRuntimeHandle and
  ExtensionRuntimeHandle that borrows tool_name/tool_call_id instead of
  requiring owned Strings — eliminates clone-per-tool-call
- Change NativeRustExtensionRuntimeHandle state from Mutex to RwLock for
  better read concurrency
- Make validate_host_call pub(crate) for use from dispatcher
- Add params_without_key helper, make dispatch_hostcall_ui/events use
  _ref variants to avoid unnecessary payload cloning
- Change hostcall_request_to_payload to call .to_string() on capability
  only at the boundary where owned String is needed
- Invalidate ctx_cache on set_current_thinking_level
- Conditional clone of event_payload based on wasm-host feature gate

extensions_js.rs:
- Change required_capability() return type from String to &'static str,
  eliminating a String allocation per hostcall capability check
- Extract builtin_tool_required_capability() for tool name -> capability
  mapping using eq_ignore_ascii_case
- Expand io_hint() tool matching to avoid allocation

extension_tools.rs:
- Switch from execute_tool() to execute_tool_ref() to pass tool name and
  call ID by reference instead of cloning

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>

files.patch:
@@ -183,9 +183,9 @@ impl Tool for ExtensionToolWrapper {
     ) -> Result<ToolOutput> {
         let result = self
             .runtime
-            .execute_tool(
-                self.def.name.clone(),
-                tool_call_id.to_string(),
+            .execute_tool_ref(
+                &self.def.name,
+                tool_call_id,
                 input,
                 Arc::clone(&self.ctx_payload),
                 self.timeout_ms,
